frameworks.name: multivoxel neural reinforcement (decoded neurofeedback)
frameworks.one_line: pairing reward with target multivoxel activation patterns to retrain affective responses

frameworks.name: resting-state functional connectivity
frameworks.one_line: stable inter-region coupling at rest as readout of network reorganization

frameworks.name: Hebbian learning
frameworks.one_line: co-activation-driven synaptic strengthening as candidate mechanism

core_question: Does decoded multivoxel neural reinforcement induce lasting functional connectivity changes within the threat regulation network?
key_assumptions: pre-vs-post resting-state connectivity indexes durable network change
ventral temporal cortex carries visual representations of phobic targets
prefrontal regions exert regulatory control over amygdala
manipulations: pre vs post neural reinforcement resting-state fMRI sessions
key_findings: increased connectivity between prefrontal regulatory regions and amygdala after reinforcement
increased connectivity between prefrontal regulatory regions and ventral temporal cortex
no evidence of Hebbian-like learning during the reinforcement procedure
interpretations: decoded multivoxel neurofeedback may engage mechanisms distinct from conventional neurofeedback
intervention produces stable network-level changes in threat regulation circuitry
limitations_noted: mechanisms behind the behavioral effect remain incompletely understood
clinical utility for treatment remains unclear
